title: AI Study Coach For University Students
description: A privacy-aware study coach architecture that grounds plans in course notes, tracks weak topics, and keeps the student in control.
user_idea: Build an AI study coach that helps me plan weekly revision, quiz myself from course notes, and track weak topics without exposing private notes.
assumptions: Course notes are private study material.
The student wants quizzes and weekly planning.
Memory should store only learning progress, not full private notes.

naive_baseline.summary: Student asks one LLM for a study plan, and the model responds from general knowledge without privacy filtering, course grounding, or approval.
failure_modes: Plans can be generic and disconnected from actual course notes.
Private notes may be copied into prompts or memory without boundaries.
The student has no approval gate before a schedule is treated as final.

nodes.name: Student Goal Input
nodes.description: Collects exam dates, weekly availability, and study goals.

outputs.name: study_goal
outputs.description: Goal, schedule constraints, and target topics.

risks.description: A vague goal can produce a weak plan.
risks.mitigation: Ask for deadline and available hours before planning.

alternatives.name: Template-based intake
alternatives.tradeoff: More structured but slower to fill out.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The coach starts by learning what the student needs to accomplish.

nodes.name: Privacy Filter
nodes.description: Removes unnecessary personal or sensitive note content before model calls.

inputs.name: course_notes
inputs.description: Private course notes and study materials.

outputs.name: minimized_notes
outputs.description: Notes with only needed study content retained.

risks.description: Removing too much context can weaken quiz quality.
risks.mitigation: Keep topic titles and learning objectives while removing personal details.

cost_estimate.notes: Can be local rules for MVP.

alternatives.name: Manual privacy review
alternatives.tradeoff: More control but more user effort.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This step keeps the system from using more private material than it needs.

nodes.name: Course Notes Knowledge Base
nodes.description: Stores minimized notes for retrieval during planning and quiz generation.

risks.description: Course notes are sensitive and need a clear storage boundary.
risks.mitigation: Store minimized notes only and support reset or deletion.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The knowledge base is the private course library the coach can search.

nodes.name: Retrieval
nodes.description: Finds relevant topics, deadlines, and notes for the current study goal.

config.fallback_when_empty: ask student for syllabus or topic list

risks.description: Wrong notes can create a poor revision plan.
risks.mitigation: Show selected topics and allow the student to adjust.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: Retrieval picks the course material that matters for this week's plan.

nodes.name: Study Plan Prompt
nodes.description: Instructs the model to make a practical, bounded revision plan.

outputs.name: planning_prompt
outputs.description: Prompt for the study coach model.

config.output_format: weekly plan with topics, quiz blocks, and rest periods
config.uncertainty_behavior: ask when deadlines or topics are missing

risks.description: A plan can assign too much work.
risks.mitigation: Require available-hours constraints and evaluator review.

cost_estimate.notes: Prompt assembly has no model cost.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The prompt turns the student's goal and notes into clear instructions for the model.

nodes.name: LLM Study Coach
nodes.description: Drafts a weekly plan and explains why each block matters.

risks.description: The model can fall back to generic study tips.
risks.mitigation: Ground the prompt in retrieved course context.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This is the part that writes the first version of the plan.

nodes.name: Quiz Generator
nodes.description: Creates short quiz questions from retrieved course topics.

risks.description: Generated quizzes may test the wrong fact.
risks.mitigation: Keep questions tied to retrieved course notes.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The quiz generator turns study material into practice questions.

nodes.name: Progress Memory
nodes.description: Stores weak topics, completed sessions, and student-approved progress state.

inputs.name: quiz_results
inputs.description: Self-reported quiz results and weak topics.

outputs.name: progress_state
outputs.description: Small memory record for future study plans.

config.retention_policy: student can reset progress at any time

risks.description: Learning progress can be sensitive personal data.
risks.mitigation: Store only weak-topic summaries with reset support.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: Memory helps the coach remember progress without keeping the full notes.

nodes.name: Overload And Clarity Evaluator
nodes.description: Checks the plan for unrealistic workload, unclear tasks, and stressful tone.

outputs.name: reviewed_plan
outputs.description: Plan with overload or clarity warnings.

risks.description: The evaluator may miss stress or unrealistic effort.
risks.mitigation: Ask the student to approve or edit before finalizing.

alternatives.name: Student-only review
alternatives.tradeoff: More control but fewer automated warnings.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This checks whether the plan is practical before the student sees it as final.

nodes.name: Student Approval
nodes.description: Lets the student edit or approve the plan before it becomes final.

inputs.name: reviewed_plan
inputs.description: Plan with warnings and quiz blocks.

risks.description: A student may approve a plan that does not fit their real week.
risks.mitigation: Make edit and reject actions equally visible.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The student remains in charge of the final schedule.

nodes.name: Final Study Plan
nodes.description: Shows the approved weekly plan, quiz blocks, and progress update instructions.

config.output_format: weekly schedule, quiz list, weak-topic tracking prompt

risks.description: The plan may look authoritative even when notes were incomplete.
risks.mitigation: Show assumptions and missing context warnings.

alternatives.name: Calendar export
alternatives.tradeoff: Useful later but adds tool permission concerns.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This is the student's approved plan, not an automatic command.
